mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects peak manufacturing output and strong corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, or the signal reverses direction during contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL correlates with positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of monthly observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll increase exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic growth and rising aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Months with payroll gains above 200k produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply-side constraints and weak business investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Falling INDPRO during recovery phases yields positive 20-day forward returns in > 65% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power outside the specified regime.
